revision.text:
{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on it as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on it as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on it as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[:en:MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on it as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[:en:MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}[[Category:reimport]]

{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on this as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on this as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FExpandContractions here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FExpandContractions for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Tool warning}} {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=ExpandContractions |desc=<onlyinclude>Expands contractions</onlyinclude> |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js]] }}
This script adds a button to the sidebar that expands contractions when editing in source mode (not [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 editor]]). Specifically, it replaces * "it's" etc. with "... is" or "... has" * "it'd" etc. with "... would" or "... had" * {{"'}}re" with " are" * {{"'}}ve" with " have" * {{"'}}ll" with " will" * "can't" with "cannot" * "won't" with "will not" * "n't" with " not"
It will not replace "I'm", "I'd", or "ain't" because they don't typically appear in encyclopedic prose (if they do, the contractions are likely the least of the article's problems). {{"'}}ll" is assumed to be short for "will" because who uses "shall" anymore—certainly not someone who leaves contractions in.
It does its best to distinguish between "is" and "has" for {{"'}}s" and between "would" and "had" for {{"'}}d", but this is not perfect so you'll be reminded to review the result if the replacements include {{"'}}s" or {{"'}}d".
Links, references, quotes, poems, hatnotes, comments, headings, etc. are excluded from replacements, but don't rely too much on this as it might still replace contractions where it shouldn't, e.g. a template parameter for a filename. Italic and bold text is also excluded because it tends to be legitimate titles, aliases, etc. Anything after a heading named "References", "Sources", "Bibliography", "Further reading", or "External links" is also ignored.
If the script incorrectly replaces a contraction inside an inline quote (or misses one outside it), it likely means the paragraph has missing or [[MOS:QWQ|misnested]] quotes (e.g. {{!xt|"... "..." ..."}} where it should be {{xt|"... '...' ..."}}).
==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]]: <syntaxhighlight lang="javascript"> mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/ExpandContractions.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); </syntaxhighlight>
Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page.
==Acknowledgements== This script relies on [[User:Pathoschild|Pathoschild]]'s [[m:TemplateScript|TemplateScript]] framework. Its escape feature was buggy, however, so the script implements its own.
{{User:Nardog/scripts}}
